title: Attached: The New Science of Adult Attachment and How It Can Help You Find—and Keep—Love
subtitle: The definitive popular introduction to attachment theory in adult relationships.
author: Amir Levine & Rachel Heller
themes: The three attachment styles: Anxious, Avoidant, and Secure
How childhood attachment patterns shape adult relationships
Why opposites attract — and why the Anxious-Avoidant trap is so common
How to identify attachment styles in yourself and partners
The science of emotional bonding and relationship security
excerpt: Why do some people seem to navigate relationships with ease while others are trapped in patterns of anxiety and avoidance? Psychiatrist Amir Levine and psychologist Rachel Heller reveal that the answer lies in attachment theory — the science of how humans bond. Drawing on decades of research, they show that adult romantic relationships are governed by the same attachment system that bonds infants to caregivers. Understanding your attachment style — and your partner's — is the key to finding and keeping love.
